license.notice: Event descriptions are paraphrased summaries written for this dataset. Source links point to authoritative references.

entries.title: RDP ClientActiveX is trying to connect to the server
entries.summary: Outbound RDP connection attempt initiated from this host.
entries.details: Generated on the source (client) machine when an outbound RDP connection attempt is initiated. Key field: server (the target hostname or IP address). This is a source-side artifact — it records where this machine connected to, making it critical for tracing lateral movement chains. During an investigation of a compromised host, EID 1024 entries reveal which other systems were targeted for RDP from this machine. Correlate the target value with LocalSessionManager EID 21 on the destination host to confirm the connection was successful.

investigationPivots: The server field identifies the RDP target — pivot to that host's LocalSessionManager and RemoteConnectionManager logs
Multiple EID 1024 entries to different targets from the same host indicate lateral movement
Correlate timestamp with LocalSessionManager EID 21 on the target host to confirm a successful session was established
commonFalsePositives: Legitimate admin activity from IT or helpdesk machines
Jump server connections to managed endpoints

entries.title: RDP ClientActiveX has been disconnected
entries.summary: Outbound RDP session ended or was disconnected.
entries.details: Generated on the source (client) machine when an RDP session is disconnected. Key fields: server (the target host) and the disconnect reason code. Common reason codes: 0 (local disconnect/user closed), 1 (remote disconnection by server), 2 (transport-level error), 3 (connection lost/network issue), 260 (DNS name lookup failed). Correlate with EID 1024 to calculate the session duration and determine whether the disconnect was user-initiated or unexpected.

investigationPivots: Pair with EID 1024 using the server field to calculate session duration
Reason code 2 or 3 (transport error / network loss) may indicate the connection was forcibly terminated
Reason code 260 (DNS failure) following EID 1024 indicates the target hostname did not resolve
commonFalsePositives: Normal end-of-session disconnects by legitimate users

entries.title: The client has initiated a multi-transport connection to the server
entries.summary: RDP multi-transport connection established; target IP recorded.
entries.details: Generated on the source (client) machine when an RDP multi-transport connection is initiated. Key field: server (IP address of the target). Unlike EID 1024 which may contain a hostname, EID 1102 typically records the resolved IP address, making it useful for cases where EID 1024 only logged a hostname. Not generated for all RDP connections — depends on whether multi-transport (UDP or additional TCP channels) is negotiated. Correlate with EID 1024 to link hostname to IP when both events are present.

investigationPivots: Use the IP address in the server field when EID 1024 only recorded a hostname — helps correlate with network logs
Not all RDP sessions generate this event — absence does not rule out an RDP connection
Correlate IP with network flow data or firewall logs to confirm the connection
commonFalsePositives: Any legitimate RDP session that uses multi-transport
